title: Keyless free verdict can hand a thin record a false 'Meets threshold / 62%' eager-yes (keyword Met; a conference talk scores Scholarly:Met)
expected: On a genuinely thin record (open-source + a couple of conference talks + comp, but no awards/no press), the free screener returns an honest 'likely not yet' that names the real gaps — never a flattering 'you meet the bar' that would lure me into paying for a draft I can't use.
got: The keyless path (the only one an unauthenticated prospect sees) is mockQualification, which marks a criterion 'Met' on keyword presence alone. A realistic Wei paste fires Original-contribution (open-source/github/stars), High-remuneration (equity/$), AND Scholarly (the regex includes the word 'conference', so a *talk* is scored as a *publication*) = 3 Met -> summarizeCriteria.meetsThreshold true -> green 'Meets threshold' badge + likelihood 38+3*8 = 62%, wrapped in Seal/Guilloche 'Certificate' framing. For a price-sensitive prospect whose whole decision hinges on honesty, a false eager-yes is the product's worst failure mode (his note: 'worse than worthless').
suggested_acceptance: Drop 'conference' (and similarly loose talk-signals) from the Scholarly-articles regex so a talk no longer scores as a publication; consider a more conservative keyless likelihood/threshold read (or render the keyword caveat inside the certificate card, not only in the gate below it) so the free path cannot show a thin record 'Meets threshold'.
l2_priority: On the authenticated LLM_ENGINE=claude path, paste Wei's exact thin profile and assert the real model returns a calibrated 'not yet' with the right gaps and does NOT count the conference talks as Scholarly:Met. Separately, the keyless mock honesty is fully visible in code and needs the fix regardless.
scope_note: Same root keyword defect Kenji logged as kw-qualify-01 (major/trust); recorded here from the inverse prospect-buyer seat (a thin record falsely inflated, not a strong one mislabeled). Overlaps backlog G3.1/PN-QUAL-01 'Certificate/Approved theater' but the live mechanism (wrong Met + green threshold badge) is sharper than branding.

title: Alt-masthead claims 'Most of our candidates meet seven' — an unsubstantiated, implausible overclaim that reads as a funnel signal
expected: Positioning never overclaims; a skeptical prospect re-reading the alt masthead for consistency finds the same sober, source-backed line as landing/FAQ — nothing he'd later feel suckered by.
got: /landing-claude states 'The statute asks for three. Most of our candidates meet seven.' — a boast with no data behind it (no candidate base on a 90-day MVP; statistically implausible that most meet 7 of 8). Beside it, all eight criteria render a decorative green check. To a distrust-primed prospect this is the exact over-eager tell that signals a lead-magnet funnel, undercutting the otherwise-disciplined copy.
suggested_acceptance: Replace the 'most of our candidates meet seven' line with a sober, true statement (e.g. 'The statute asks for three of eight; the screener scores all eight against your record'). Not present in accepted-gaps/BACKLOG.
l2_priority: Low — copy honesty is fully visible in code; L2 only to confirm no other surface repeats the claim.
scope_note: Reachable from the main footer ('Alt. masthead'); the character is explicitly told to re-read this surface for consistency.

title: Share affordance mounts on any hero result, so a certificate minted before sign-in can encode the keyword mock's inflated Met statuses (and the OG card itself carries no not-legal-advice line)
expected: What I share is credible and accurate; a positive share reflects a real read, not a keyword artifact, and the unfurled social card stays within the informational/not-legal-advice frame.
got: LettersPatentShare renders under every hero (keyless mock) result, so a 'Meets threshold / 62%' certificate built from the inflated keyword scores (including talk-as-Scholarly:Met) is fully shareable before any model read. Privacy is intact (no profile text in the token), but the *content* can be the mock's false Met. Separately, the OG social image carries only 'Screen yourself free' with no 'informational / not legal advice' line (the /c/[token] page has it; the unfurled card does not).
suggested_acceptance: Fixing WZ-QUAL-01 at the source removes the inflated statuses from any minted token. Optionally add the 'informational / not legal advice' line to the OG image for share-context parity.
l2_priority: Verify a shared keyless-mock certificate reflects honest statuses once the keyword heuristic is fixed; confirm OG card renders.
scope_note: Same root cause as WZ-QUAL-01; share machinery itself (codec, privacy, framing) is sound.

title: STRENGTH: free read has NO signup/paywall, and the build is honest that the keyless path is a shallow keyword scan
expected: An honest free verdict with no email-harvest before value, that does not pretend the cheap read is the deep one.
got: The hero form takes name(optional)+visa+profile and renders the result inline — no email field, no account gate. The result badge reads 'Template' (neutral tone) and the SoftGate states 'This was an instant keyword read. The full screening reads your whole record in depth.' This directly defuses the prospect's funnel/upsell fear and satisfies AC #1's no-signup/paywall clause.
suggested_acceptance: Protect: keep the no-wall free read and the honest 'Template'/'keyword read' labeling.

title: STRENGTH: unscored ('None') criteria render neutral AND are excluded from the qualifying count via one shared classifier — never fake-green
expected: Unscored criteria render neutral, never green, and never inflate the odds; the verdict is legible (X of 8, need 3), not a black-box percentage.
got: classifyStatus is the single source of truth: 'None'/unknown -> 'other' -> statusTone 'neutral' AND excluded from summarizeCriteria's qualifying/total, so the table tone and the threshold count cannot disagree. The report shows 'X of 8 criteria supported - need 3 to qualify'. Wei's three honest empties (Awards/Membership/Press) will not green-pad his odds. Satisfies AC #2's never-green + legibility clauses.
suggested_acceptance: Protect: keep the single shared classifier feeding both tone and count.

title: STRENGTH: pricing/positioning canonical and consistent (no drift, no contact-sales wall on the core offer); validation page shows real primary sources
expected: Transparent self-serve price obviously better than a $300-$600 consult, consistent positioning across surfaces, and credibility from real sources rather than adjectives.
got: Per-op (qualify 3 / draft 12 / RFE 5 / categorize 1) and bundle ($5+) prices are derived from registry.ts/economy.ts and are identical across landing, FAQ, and /billing - they cannot drift. The only contact-sales is the Enterprise band; the core offer is fully self-serve with 150 free tokens. /validation lists each program with a legal basis and primary/agency sources (eCFR, Cornell LII, USCIS Policy Manual, AZ Supreme Court order PDF) plus dated review and freshness, CI-gated. FAQ answers what/who-signs/cost/legal-advice/refunds/security straight. Satisfies AC #4 and #5.
suggested_acceptance: Protect: keep prices sourced from the canonical registry/economy; keep the validation page sourced from the real validation layer.

title: STRENGTH: share token leaks no profile text, is DB-free and pack-validated, with honest 'Qualifies/In progress' framing
expected: A share I'd actually send - credible, informational (not a legal grant), with nothing private leaking into the token/URL.
got: encodeSnapshot carries only name/classification/likelihood/per-criterion-status; the pasted profile never enters the token. /c/[token] and the OG image render from the token alone (no DB); decodeSnapshot rejects a non-live program or a status count that doesn't match the live pack. The cert stamps 'Qualifies' only at/above threshold else 'In progress', reuses statusTone (None stays neutral), and carries 'Informational only - not legal advice'. Satisfies AC #6.
suggested_acceptance: Protect: keep the snapshot codec (no profile text) and the pack-validated, threshold-honest framing.
